message:
chore(release): bump version to 0.2.0

Update version numbers in preparation for v0.2.0 release:
- VERSION file: 0.1.0 → 0.2.0
- ASB_VERSION constant in asb script

v0.2.0 includes: dry-run mode, restore confirmation, config file
support, export/import archives, and shell completions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -2,7 +2,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-170","title":"Export: Register export and import commands in main()","description":"# Register export and import Commands

## Task

Add export and import to the command dispatch in main().

## Implementation

Add to the case statement in main():

```bash
case "$command" in
    backup|b)
        backup_all "$@"
        ;;
    restore|r)
        if [[ $# -lt 1 ]]; then
            log_error "Usage: asb restore <agent> [commit]"
            exit 1
        fi
        restore_agent "$@"
        ;;
    export|ex)
        if [[ $# -lt 1 ]]; then
            log_error "Usage: asb export <agent> [file]"
            exit 1
        fi
        local agent="$1"
        shift
        if ! agent_exists "$agent"; then
            log_error "Unknown agent: ${agent}"
            log_info "Run 'asb list' to see available agents"
            exit 1
        fi
        export_backup "$agent" "$@"
        ;;
    import|im)
        if [[ $# -lt 1 ]]; then
            log_error "Usage: asb import <file>"
            exit 1
        fi
        import_backup "$@"
        ;;
    # ... rest of commands ...
esac
```

## Command Aliases

- `export` or `ex` for export
- `import` or `im` for import

Short aliases follow the pattern of other commands (backup/b, restore/r, etc.)

## Argument Validation

- export requires agent name, file is optional (defaults to timestamped name)
- import requires archive path
- Both support "-" for stdin/stdout","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:25:57.453450315Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T04:00:18.309698091Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T04:00:18.309588985Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-170","depends_on_id":"bd-29l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:29:06.084345918Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-170","depends_on_id":"bd-3jx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:29:06.258621043Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-170","depends_on_id":"bd-v3p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:25:57.464457247Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-18r","title":"Dry-run: Modify backup_agent for dry-run mode","description":"# Modify backup_agent for Dry-Run Mode

## Task

Add dry-run handling to backup_agent() function.

## Implementation

Add early return at the start of backup_agent():

```bash
backup_agent() {
    local agent="$1"
    local source
    local dest
    local agent_name="${AGENT_NAMES[$agent]:-$agent}"

    source=$(get_agent_source "$agent")
    dest=$(get_agent_backup_dir "$agent")

    if [[ ! -d "$source" ]]; then
        log_warn "${agent_name} not found at ${source}"
        return 1
    fi

    # === DRY RUN MODE ===
    if [[ "$DRY_RUN" == "true" ]]; then
        log_info "${DIM}[DRY RUN]${RESET} Would backup ${agent_name}"
        log_info "${DIM}[DRY RUN]${RESET} Source: ${source}"
        log_info "${DIM}[DRY RUN]${RESET} Destination: ${dest}"
        
        # Show what files would change
        if command_exists rsync; then
            local changes
            changes=$(rsync -a --dry-run --itemize-changes \
                --exclude='.git' \
                --exclude='.gitignore' \
                --exclude='*.log' \
                --exclude='cache/' \
                --exclude='Cache/' \
                --exclude='.cache/' \
                --exclude='*.sqlite3-wal' \
                --exclude='*.sqlite3-shm' \
                "$source/" "$dest/" 2>/dev/null | head -30)
            
            if [[ -n "$changes" ]]; then
                log_info "${DIM}[DRY RUN]${RESET} Files that would be synced:"
                echo "$changes" | while read -r line; do
                    echo "  $line"
                done
            else
                log_info "${DIM}[DRY RUN]${RESET} No file changes detected"
            fi
        fi
        
        # Check if commit would be created
        if [[ "${ASB_AUTO_COMMIT:-true}" == "true" ]]; then
            if [[ -d "$dest/.git" ]]; then
                log_info "${DIM}[DRY RUN]${RESET} Would create backup commit"
            else
                log_info "${DIM}[DRY RUN]${RESET} Would initialize git repo and create initial commit"
            fi
        fi
        
        return 0
    fi
    # === END DRY RUN MODE ===

    # ... rest of existing implementation unchanged ...
}
```

## rsync Itemize Changes

The `--itemize-changes` flag produces output like:
```
>f..t...... settings.json      # file, timestamp changed
>f.st...... config.yaml        # file, size and timestamp changed  
.d..t...... projects/          # directory, timestamp changed
```

This gives users detailed insight into what would be synced.

## Edge Cases

- If destination doesn't exist yet, rsync will show all files as new
- We limit output to 30 lines to avoid overwhelming output
- We check if git repo exists to determine "would initialize" vs "would commit"","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:23:29.973427440Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T04:29:20.732360235Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T04:29:20.732246421Z","close_reason":"backup_agent dry-run already implemented; cleaned redundant is_dry_run check and used is_truthy for commit preview","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-18r","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:23:29.988384823Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-18r","depends_on_id":"bd-2lk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:28:44.944634479Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1as","title":"Dry-run: Update help text with dry-run flag","description":"# Update Help Text for Dry-Run

## Task

Update show_help() to document the --dry-run flag.

## Changes

Add to USAGE section:
```bash
${BOLD}USAGE${RESET}
    asb [OPTIONS] <command> [args]

${BOLD}OPTIONS${RESET}
    -n, --dry-run    Show what would happen without making changes
    -v, --verbose    Show detailed debug output
```

Add to EXAMPLES section:
```bash
${BOLD}EXAMPLES${RESET}
    asb backup                  # Backup all detected agents
    asb --dry-run backup        # Preview backup without changes
    asb -n backup claude        # Preview backup of specific agent
    asb backup claude codex     # Backup specific agents
    # ... existing examples ...
```

## Also Update

Update the brief description at the top of help:
```
Backs up AI coding agent configuration folders to git-versioned repositories.
Use --dry-run to preview changes before making them.
```","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:23:30.382737214Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T04:03:26.366796882Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T04:03:26.366673219Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1as","depends_on_id":"bd-18r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:28:45.447556717Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1as","depends_on_id":"bd-21c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:28:45.600537845Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1as","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:23:30.398229264Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1as","depends_on_id":"bd-g6l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:28:45.763617753Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1gy","title":"Manual Acceptance Testing: Human verification checklist","description":"# Manual Acceptance Testing: Human Verification Checklist

## Purpose
This bead tracks MANUAL acceptance testing that requires human judgment and verification. This complements the automated unit tests and E2E tests.

## When to Use This Checklist
- After all automated tests pass
- Before release
- When testing subjective qualities (UX, messaging clarity)

## Manual Verification Checklist

### User Experience

#### Configuration File UX
- [ ] `asb config init` output is clear and helpful
- [ ] `asb config show` is well-formatted and readable
- [ ] Error messages for invalid config are helpful

#### Dry-Run UX
- [ ] Dry-run output clearly indicates no changes made
- [ ] Preview shows enough detail to understand what WOULD happen
- [ ] Colors are helpful (not distracting)

#### Conflict Detection UX
- [ ] Preview of restore changes is easy to understand
- [ ] Deletions are CLEARLY marked (users must understand data loss)
- [ ] Confirmation prompt is unambiguous
- [ ] "y to proceed, any other key to cancel" is clear

#### Export/Import UX
- [ ] Archive filename is sensible (date-based)
- [ ] Import prompts before overwriting are clear
- [ ] Progress indication for large archives

#### Shell Completion UX
- [ ] Completion instructions in help are easy to follow
- [ ] Completions feel natural and responsive
- [ ] No unexpected completions

### Help Text Review
- [ ] All new commands documented in `asb --help`
- [ ] All new flags documented
- [ ] Examples are accurate and work
- [ ] No typos or grammatical errors

### Edge Cases (Manual Verification)
- [ ] What happens with no backup directory?
- [ ] What happens with no git installed?
- [ ] What happens with read-only filesystem?
- [ ] What happens with very long filenames?
- [ ] What happens with special characters in filenames?
- [ ] What happens on Windows Subsystem for Linux?

### Backward Compatibility
- [ ] Existing backups from v0.1 still work
- [ ] No breaking changes to existing commands
- [ ] Upgrade path is smooth

### Documentation Review
- [ ] README.md is accurate and complete
- [ ] All examples work as shown
- [ ] Installation instructions are correct
- [ ] Screenshots (if any) are up to date

## Test Environments
Test manually on:
- [ ] Ubuntu/Debian
- [ ] macOS (if available)
- [ ] Different terminal emulators

## Sign-off
- [ ] Developer self-review complete
- [ ] All automated tests passing
- [ ] All manual checks above verified
- [ ] Ready for release

## Notes
Automated tests are in separate beads:
- bd-3ig: Test Infrastructure
- bd-9rp: Unit Test: Configuration File
- bd-3hq: Unit Test: Dry-Run Mode
- bd-shi: Unit Test: Conflict Detection
- bd-k47: Unit Test: Import/Export
- bd-34i: Unit Test: Shell Completion
- bd-3sg: E2E Test Suite","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:27:57.355820416Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T21:41:46.771896303Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1gy","depends_on_id":"bd-3sg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:41:46.771879Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1gy","depends_on_id":"bd-3u9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:27:57.370667010Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

","created_at":"2026-01-21T08:10:40Z"},{"id":2,"issue_id":"bd-1gy","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Manual acceptance checks (local, Linux env):

Completed:
- `asb config init` output is clear; config path printed and success message.
- `asb config show` formatting readable; shows effective values and precedence line.
- Dry-run backup output clearly marked `[DRY RUN]` and shows source/dest + summary.
- Restore preview is clear; added/replace/delete entries are color-coded and warning banner appears for deletions.
- Confirmation prompt is explicit: `Proceed with restore? [y/N]` (verified via TTY run, cancelled with `n`).
- Export naming is date-based (`claude-backup-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.tar.gz`) and success output shows size/history.
- Import prompt on existing backup is clear: `Overwrite existing backup? [y/N]` (verified; cancel works).
- Help text and completion instructions look accurate; completion output begins with correct install hints.

Notes/limitations:
- macOS/WSL-specific behavior not tested here.
- Read-only filesystem, long/special filenames not tested.
- Backward compatibility with v0.1 backups not verified (no legacy backups available).
- In a fresh env without git user config, initial commit can fail unless git author/committer is set; typical user git config likely covers this.

 {"id":"bd-1he","title":"Export: Update help text with export/import commands","description":"# Update Help Text for Export/Import

## Task

Update show_help() to document the new export and import commands.

## Changes to COMMANDS section

```bash
${BOLD}COMMANDS${RESET}
    backup [agents...]        Backup agent settings (all if none specified)
    restore <agent> [commit]  Restore agent from backup (prompts for confirmation)
    export <agent> [file]     Export backup as portable archive
    import <file>             Import backup from archive
    list                      List all agents and backup status
    history <agent>           Show backup history for an agent
    diff <agent>              Show changes since last backup
    init                      Initialize backup location
    config [init|show]        Manage configuration
    help                      Show this help message
    version                   Show version
```

## Add PORTABILITY section

```bash
${BOLD}PORTABILITY${RESET}
    Export backups as portable archives for transfer between machines:
    
    asb export claude                    # Creates claude-backup-DATE.tar.gz
    asb export claude my-backup.tar.gz   # Custom filename
    
    Import archives:
    
    asb import backup.tar.gz             # Import to backup location
    
    Piping support (for remote transfer, encryption, cloud storage):
    
    asb export claude - | ssh host "asb import -"
    asb export claude - | gpg -c > backup.gpg
    asb export claude - | aws s3 cp - s3://bucket/backup.tar.gz
```

## Update EXAMPLES section

```bash
${BOLD}EXAMPLES${RESET}
    # ... existing examples ...
    
    # Export and import
    asb export claude                  # Export Claude backup
    asb import claude-backup.tar.gz    # Import on another machine
    
    # Transfer to remote machine
    asb export claude - | ssh server "asb import -"
```","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:25:57.584866864Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T04:03:25.927621853Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T04:03:25.927493371Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1he","depends_on_id":"bd-170","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:29:06.415575895Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1he","depends_on_id":"bd-v3p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:25:57.594324187Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

## Task

## Changes to COMMANDS section

```bash

    Config file: ~/.config/asb/config (XDG-compliant)

    View settings:    asb config show

```

```bash

    ASB_AUTO_COMMIT       Auto-commit on backup (default: true)
    ASB_VERBOSE           Verbose output (default: false)
    
    Tip: Use 'asb config init' to create a persistent config file.
```","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:22:22.540545002Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T04:03:25.694712487Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T04:03:25.694575599Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1i7","depends_on_id":"bd-1lm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:22:22.557272940Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1i7","depends_on_id":"bd-2jp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:28:33.532835015Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1i7","depends_on_id":"bd-2xn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:28:33.693316049Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1j1","title":"Config: Add get_config_dir and get_config_file functions","description":"# Add Config Path Functions

## Task

Add two utility functions to determine config file locations following XDG Base Directory spec.

## Implementation

```bash
get_config_dir() {
    echo "${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-$HOME/.config}/asb"
}

get_config_file() {
    echo "$(get_config_dir)/config"
}
```

## Placement

Add these in the UTILITIES section, after `get_agent_backup_dir()`.

## XDG Compliance

The XDG Base Directory Specification says:
- $XDG_CONFIG_HOME defines the base directory for user-specific config files
- If $XDG_CONFIG_HOME is not set, default to $HOME/.config

This ensures asb plays nicely with users who have custom XDG setups.

## Testing

```bash
# Default case
unset XDG_CONFIG_HOME
./asb # should use ~/.config/asb/config

# Custom XDG
export XDG_CONFIG_HOME=/tmp/myconfig
./asb # should use /tmp/myconfig/asb/config
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:22:22.134317393Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T03:49:18.458240562Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T03:49:18.457922804Z","close_reason":"Already implemented get_config_dir/get_config_file in asb utilities","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1j1","depends_on_id":"bd-1lm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:22:22.149049803Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
@@ -35,7 +35,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-9rp","title":"Unit Test: Configuration File Support tests","description":"# Unit Test: Configuration File Support

## Purpose
Create an automated test script (tests/test_config.sh) that verifies all configuration file functionality.

## Test Script Requirements

### Setup/Teardown
- Create isolated test environment with custom XDG_CONFIG_HOME
- Clean up test artifacts after each test
- Support --verbose flag for detailed logging

### Test Cases

1. **test_config_init_creates_file**
   - Run `asb config init`
   - Verify file created at expected location
   - Verify file has valid YAML structure
   - Verify default values present

2. **test_config_init_idempotent**
   - Create config with custom value
   - Run `asb config init` again
   - Verify original file NOT overwritten

3. **test_config_show_displays_all**
   - Create config with known values
   - Run `asb config show`
   - Verify all settings displayed

4. **test_config_precedence**
   - Set value in config file
   - Set same value via env var
   - Verify config file wins

5. **test_missing_config_graceful**
   - Ensure no config file exists
   - Run `asb backup --dry-run`
   - Verify no errors, defaults used

6. **test_xdg_config_home_respected**
   - Set custom XDG_CONFIG_HOME
   - Run `asb config init`
   - Verify file in custom location

7. **test_malformed_config_error**
   - Create invalid YAML config
   - Run `asb config show`
   - Verify helpful error message

## Logging Requirements
- Each test logs: START, PASS/FAIL, duration

 {"id":"bd-g6l","title":"Dry-run: Add dry-run support to restore command","description":"# Add Dry-Run Support to Restore

## Task

Add dry-run handling to restore_agent() function.

## Implementation

In restore_agent(), add dry-run handling after validation:

```bash
restore_agent() {
    local agent="$1"
    local commit="${2:-HEAD}"
    local source
    local dest
    local agent_name="${AGENT_NAMES[$agent]:-$agent}"

    source=$(get_agent_backup_dir "$agent")
    dest=$(get_agent_source "$agent")

    if [[ ! -d "$source/.git" ]]; then
        log_error "No backup found for ${agent_name}"
        return 1
    fi

    # === DRY RUN MODE ===
    if [[ "$DRY_RUN" == "true" ]]; then
        log_info "${DIM}[DRY RUN]${RESET} Would restore ${agent_name}"
        log_info "${DIM}[DRY RUN]${RESET} From: ${source}"
        log_info "${DIM}[DRY RUN]${RESET} To: ${dest}"
        log_info "${DIM}[DRY RUN]${RESET} Commit: ${commit}"
        
        # Show what would change
        if [[ -d "$dest" ]]; then
            log_info "${DIM}[DRY RUN]${RESET} Changes that would be applied:"
            # Create temp with backup state for comparison
            local temp_backup
            temp_backup=$(mktemp -d)
            trap "rm -rf '$temp_backup'" RETURN
            git -C "$source" archive "$commit" 2>/dev/null | tar -C "$temp_backup" -xf - 2>/dev/null
            
            diff -rq "$dest" "$temp_backup" \
                --exclude='.git' \
                --exclude='.gitignore' \
                2>/dev/null | head -20 || log_info "  (no differences or comparison unavailable)"
        else
            log_info "${DIM}[DRY RUN]${RESET} Would create ${dest} with backup contents"
        fi
        
        return 0
    fi
    # === END DRY RUN MODE ===

    # ... rest of existing implementation ...
}
```

## Why This Matters

Restore is even MORE important to preview than backup:
- Restore overwrites current settings
- Wrong commit selection could lose recent changes
- Dry-run lets user verify they're restoring the right thing","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:23:30.240620781Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T04:29:23.299100084Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T04:29:23.299019573Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-g6l","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:23:30.256884094Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-g6l","depends_on_id":"bd-2lk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:28:45.285629690Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-k47","title":"Unit Test: Import/Export Archive tests","description":"# Unit Test: Import/Export Archives

## Purpose
Create an automated test script (tests/test_export.sh) that verifies archive creation and restoration.

## Test Script Requirements

### Setup/Teardown
- Create isolated backup directories with git history
- Clean up archive files after tests
- Support --verbose flag for detailed logging

### Test Cases

1. **test_export_creates_archive**
   - Setup backup with git history
   - Run `asb export agent`
   - Verify .tar.gz file created
   - Verify filename is timestamped

2. **test_export_custom_filename**
   - Run `asb export agent custom.tar.gz`
   - Verify custom.tar.gz created
   - Verify no timestamped file created

3. **test_export_includes_git**
   - Export backup
   - Extract archive to temp dir
   - Verify .git directory exists
   - Verify `git log` shows history

4. **test_export_to_stdout**
   - Run `asb export agent -`
   - Pipe to tar -tzf -
   - Verify valid tar output
   - Verify contains expected files

5. **test_import_restores_backup**
   - Export a backup
   - Delete original backup dir
   - Run `asb import archive.tar.gz`
   - Verify backup dir restored
   - Verify git history intact

6. **test_import_detects_existing**
   - Export backup
   - Run `asb import archive.tar.gz` (without deleting)
   - Verify prompt about existing backup
   - Verify stdin='n' cancels

7. **test_import_force_overwrites**
   - Setup existing backup
   - Create archive with different content
   - Run `asb --force import archive.tar.gz`
   - Verify overwritten without prompt

8. **test_export_dryrun**
   - Run `asb --dry-run export agent`
   - Verify NO archive created
   - Verify output shows what WOULD be created

9. **test_import_invalid_archive**
   - Create invalid tar file
   - Run `asb import invalid.tar.gz`
   - Verify helpful error message
   - Verify exit code non-zero

10. **test_export_nonexistent_agent**
    - Run `asb export nonexistent`
    - Verify helpful error message
    - Verify lists valid agents

## Archive Verification
- Use tar -tzf to list contents
- Verify .git/objects directory present
- Check file permissions preserved

## Logging Requirements
- Each test logs: START, PASS/FAIL, duration
- Log archive contents on verification
- Show size of archive created","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:39:31.836255005Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T04:33:21.414343903Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T04:33:21.414263222Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k47","depends_on_id":"bd-3ig","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:41:26.482449288Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-k47","depends_on_id":"bd-v3p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:41:26.693568016Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-shi","title":"Unit Test: Conflict Detection tests","description":"# Unit Test: Conflict Detection on Restore

## Purpose
Create an automated test script (tests/test_conflict.sh) that verifies restore safety and conflict detection.

## Test Script Requirements

### Setup/Teardown
- Create isolated test directories
- Mock TTY/non-TTY environments
- Support --verbose flag for detailed logging

### Test Cases

1. **test_restore_shows_preview**
   - Create backup with files A, B, C
   - Create source with files A (modified), D
   - Run `asb restore agent` (capture output)
   - Verify preview shows:
     - A: will be overwritten
     - B, C: will be added
     - D: will be DELETED (critical!)

2. **test_restore_requires_confirmation**
   - Setup scenario with changes
   - Run `asb restore agent` with stdin='n'
   - Verify restore CANCELLED
   - Verify source dir UNCHANGED

3. **test_restore_confirm_yes**
   - Setup scenario with changes
   - Run `asb restore agent` with stdin='y'
   - Verify restore COMPLETED
   - Verify files match backup

4. **test_restore_enter_cancels**
   - Run `asb restore agent` with stdin='' (just Enter)
   - Verify restore CANCELLED
   - Verify source dir UNCHANGED

5. **test_force_skips_confirmation**
   - Setup scenario
   - Run `asb --force restore agent` (no stdin)
   - Verify restore COMPLETED immediately

6. **test_non_tty_without_force_error**
   - Run `asb restore agent </dev/null`
   - Verify helpful error message
   - Verify suggests --force flag
   - Verify exit code non-zero

7. **test_preview_formatting**
   - Test with many files
   - Verify colors (+ green, - red, ~ yellow)
   - Verify sorted output
   - Verify file counts in summary

8. **test_no_changes_scenario**
   - Setup source identical to backup
   - Run `asb restore agent`
   - Verify "Already up to date" message
   - Verify no confirmation needed

## TTY Mocking
Use `script` command or pty tools to test TTY behavior:
\`\`\`bash
# Force TTY
script -q -c "asb restore agent" /dev/null <<< "y"
# Force non-TTY
asb restore agent </dev/null
\`\`\`

## Logging Requirements
- Each test logs: START, PASS/FAIL, duration
- Log full command output on failure
- Show file diffs on unexpected changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:39:15.543281663Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T04:00:08.071852502Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T04:00:08.071794102Z","close_reason":"Added comprehensive tests/test_conflict.sh covering preview, confirmation paths, non-tty errors, formatting, and sorting","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-shi","depends_on_id":"bd-3co","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:41:25.109468820Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-shi","depends_on_id":"bd-3ig","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:41:24.936577217Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-v3p","title":"Feature: Import/Export Archives","description":"# Feature: Import/Export Archives

## Problem Statement

Backups that can't be moved are only half useful. Real-world scenarios where portability matters:

1. **New machine setup**: User gets a new laptop, wants to bring agent configs
2. **Sharing configs**: Team wants to share a known-good Claude setup
3. **Disaster recovery**: User's home directory is corrupted, but they emailed an archive
4. **Cross-platform**: User switches from Linux to Mac
5. **Backup redundancy**: User wants to store backups in cloud storage

Without export/import, users must manually tar directories, figure out paths, and hope for the best.

## Solution

Add two new commands:
- `asb export <agent> [file]` - Creates portable tar.gz archive with full git history
- `asb import <file>` - Restores archive to backup location

## Technical Design

### Export Command

```bash
asb export claude                    # Creates claude-backup-20240115.tar.gz
asb export claude my-backup.tar.gz   # Creates my-backup.tar.gz
asb export claude -                  # Writes to stdout (for piping)
```

### Import Command

```bash
asb import my-backup.tar.gz          # Imports to backup location
asb import -                         # Reads from stdin (for piping)
```

### Archive Format

The archive is a standard tar.gz containing:
```
.claude/
├── .git/           # Full git history preserved!
├── .gitignore
├── settings.json
└── ...
```

The directory name tells us which agent it is.

### Implementation

**export_backup()**
```bash
export_backup() {
    local agent="$1"
    local output="${2:-}"
    local agent_name="${AGENT_NAMES[$agent]:-$agent}"
    local source
    source=$(get_agent_backup_dir "$agent")
    
    if [[ ! -d "$source/.git" ]]; then
        log_error "No backup found for ${agent_name}"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Generate default filename if not specified
    if [[ -z "$output" ]]; then
        output="${agent}-backup-$(date +%Y%m%d).tar.gz"
    fi
    
    log_step "Exporting ${agent_name} to ${output}..."
    
    # Create archive preserving git history
    local backup_root
    backup_root=$(get_backup_root)
    local folder="${AGENT_FOLDERS[$agent]}"
    
    if [[ "$output" == "-" ]]; then
        # Write to stdout
        tar -czf - -C "$backup_root" "$folder"
    else
        tar -czf "$output" -C "$backup_root" "$folder"
        
        local size
        size=$(du -h "$output" | cut -f1)
        log_success "Exported ${agent_name} (${size})"
        log_info "File: $(realpath "$output")"
    fi
}
```

**import_backup()**
```bash
import_backup() {
    local archive="$1"
    local backup_root
    backup_root=$(get_backup_root)
    
    if [[ "$archive" != "-" ]] && [[ ! -f "$archive" ]]; then
        log_error "Archive not found: ${archive}"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_step "Importing from ${archive}..."
    
    # Create backup location if needed
    mkdir -p "$backup_root"
    
    # Extract archive
    if [[ "$archive" == "-" ]]; then
        tar -xzf - -C "$backup_root"
    else
        tar -xzf "$archive" -C "$backup_root"
    fi
    
    log_success "Imported backup to ${backup_root}"
    log_info "Run 'asb list' to see imported backups"
}
```

## Important Considerations

### Git History Preservation

The entire .git directory is included in the archive. This means:
- Full backup history is preserved
- Can be used immediately after import
- No need to re-initialize or lose history

### Conflict Handling

If the agent already has a backup at the destination:
- Show warning
- Require --force to overwrite
- Or prompt for confirmation in interactive mode

### Stdin/Stdout Support

The "-" argument enables piping:
```bash
# Remote transfer
asb export claude - | ssh remote "asb import -"

# S3 backup
asb export claude - | aws s3 cp - s3://bucket/claude-backup.tar.gz

# Encryption
asb export claude - | gpg -c > claude-backup.tar.gz.gpg
```

## Testing Checklist

- [ ] Export creates valid tar.gz
- [ ] Export includes .git directory
- [ ] Import extracts to correct location
- [ ] Imported backup works with asb history
- [ ] Default filename includes date
- [ ] Custom filename works
- [ ] Stdout/stdin piping works
- [ ] Dry-run shows what would be exported/imported
- [ ] Force flag handles conflicts","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:25:14.535112113Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T04:25:47.494362932Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T04:25:47.494272021Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-v3p","depends_on_id":"bd-3u9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:25:14.550127646Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
